pattern: Tracking only the current depth as a single counter is enough to answer 'how far from the root', since going up, staying put, or going down each change that counter by a fixed, predictable amount regardless of the folder names involved.
hints: You never need to know the actual folder names, only how deep you currently are.
Three operations each have a fixed effect on depth: moving to the parent decreases it (but never below the root), staying decreases nothing, and moving into a named folder increases it.
Scan the operations once, clamping depth at zero whenever a parent-move would otherwise take it negative, and return the final depth.

edge_inputs.description: a simple descent and one step back
descends deep then returns fully to the root
a single stay-in-place operation from the root
trying to go above the root should clamp at zero, not go negative
many alternating up and down moves that should mostly cancel out
descend then go up one more time than allowed at a shallow point

stress.description: long random sequence of the three operation kinds
